description: The Russian Cyrillic alphabet, learned by relating each letter to the Latin alphabet — and watching for the false friends. Each item is tested both ways, tracked separately.
primer:
## How this set works
The 33 letters of Russian Cyrillic, grouped by how they relate to the Latin alphabet. Each card is studied **both ways** and tracked separately — you only master it once you know it in both directions. The fastest path is to lean on what you already know and then beat the **false friends**.

## Same as Latin (shape & sound)
- А a · К k · М m · О o · Т t

## False friends (Latin-looking, different sound) — the ones that trip everyone
- В **v** (not b) · Е **ye** · Н **n** (not h) · Р **r**, rolled (not p) · С **s** (not c) · У **u** (not y) · Х **kh** (not x)

## New shapes
- Б b · Г g · Д d · Ё yo · Ж zh · З z · И i (ee) · Й y (short) · Л l · П p · Ф f · Ц ts · Ч ch · Ш sh · Щ shch · Э e · Ю yu · Я ya

## The two signs (no sound of their own)
- Ъ **hard sign** — keeps the preceding consonant hard before a vowel.
- Ь **soft sign** — softens (palatalises) the preceding consonant.
- Ы — a back vowel with no English equivalent (a tense 'i').

Every card's answer is in this chart — know it both ways and you'll pass the deck.
